sections.paragraph:
δαιμονικός, ή, όν (Plut.; Physiogn. I 345, 8; 12; Cat. Cod. Astr. X 112, 10; Proclus on Pla., Cratyl. p. 93, 9 Pasqu.; Ath. 25, 3; Clem. Al., Strom. 6, 12, 98; TestSol 1:2 PQ) pert. to being like a spirit or phantom, like spirits/phantoms, ghost-like w. ἀσώματος of ‘unbelieving’ teachers ISm 2; both adjectives are chosen because of the saying in 3:2.—DELG s.v. δαίμων.

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Physiogn. Physiogn. = Scriptores Physiognomici, various dates—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Clem. Al. Clem. Al. = Clement of Alexandria, II–III a.d.—List 5

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 210
